title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points, including 8-for-15 from three, leading Spurs to a 125-116 win over Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was ejected after two technical fouls, impacting the Celtics' scoring in the Spurs game.
Derrick White led the Celtics with 34 points, effectively challenging Wembanyama and providing crucial offense.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Colonial in Abington built in 1900 sold for $555,000 on a 26,553-square-foot lot.
An Andover contemporary home with 5 bedrooms and 4 baths on a nearly 40,000-square-foot lot sold for $1,778,500.
A Boston one-family row-middle with 6,682 square feet and 9 baths sold for $22,000,000.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal 39 seconds into overtime, leading Bruins to a 2-1 victory over the Kings.
Despite mouth injuries requiring dental work, McAvoy returned in the third period, contributing to Bruins' 13th straight home win.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm praised McAvoy's toughness, suggesting it could earn him a leadership promotion despite recent team changes.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected with 3:38 left in the second quarter after receiving two technical fouls.
Brown criticized referees on social media moments after his ejection, expressing frustration over officiating.
Boston Celtics lost 125-116 to the San Antonio Spurs; Brown had eight points in 15 minutes before ejection.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life with parole possibility after 17 years for murdering Margarita Morehead, 59, in Chelsea.
Alvarez pled guilty to second-degree murder, assault, and violating a protection order in February 2026, for the 2023 crime.
Alvarez fled after the stabbing; a neighbor's delayed response led to contacting police via a domestic violence hotline.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The MBTA assault occurred on February 27 at 8 p.m. in Prudential station, involving a Green Line rider.
The suspect reportedly spat on and punched the victim multiple times, wearing all black with distinct tattoos.
MBTA police urge public to anonymously share any information about the case at 617-222-1050.

title: Maine man allegedly shot and killed girlfriend; pair worked together at ski resort
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Austin Doucette, 24, is charged with murder for allegedly shooting his girlfriend, Makayla DeSantis, 23, in Carrabassett Valley.
DeSantis was shot at a condo complex for Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort employees and later died from her injuries.
The couple worked at Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort in Maine; DeSantis' family has raised over $10,000 for her burial.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million.
The team remains interested in trading for Eagles receiver A.J. Brown despite signing Doubs.
Philadelphia reportedly wants a 2027 first-round and 2026 second-round pick for A.J. Brown.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, of Fort Lauderdale is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Ellie Fund.
The Ellie Fund provides transportation, meals, childcare, and support for breast cancer patients and their families.
Sydney's participation honors her mother, a breast cancer survivor, and highlights the Fund's mission to alleviate patient burdens.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police investigated bomb threats at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain and Allston; no suspicious items found.
Reading police responded to a bomb threat at Stop & Shop on Walkers Brook Drive; the threat was deemed not credible.
Investigations continue to determine if the bomb threats at three Stop & Shop locations were related.
